Class of 1897 — Heritage Jersey

The
Materials

Every component of Class of 1897 must be traceable to the world of the historical football jersey. Sustainability is therefore not used as a marketing claim, but as a coherent consequence of the project's design logic.

Fabric

rPET Piqué

Recycled polyester

Weight

220 g/m²

Post-consumer PET bottles

Production

Italian

Short-chain, low-impact

The Fabric

Recycled.
Purposeful.

Class of 1897 is made from recycled polyester piqué, 220 g/m², obtained from post-consumer PET bottles. The piqué texture recalls classic football jerseys and polos, while the heavier weight gives the garment more structure, durability and a streetwear fit.

Enzyme washing and controlled sun-bleaching create a softer, slightly pre-worn effect. The garment also includes recycled technical mesh inserts under the arms, along the sides or near the collar — improving breathability and reinforcing the hybrid identity of the product: part football jersey, part streetwear garment.

The Label

Every Detail
Is A Document.

Each piece includes an internal organic cotton label with the wording "Collective Euphoria — Juventus F.C. Since 1897", and an external patch identifying the annual edition, the reference year, the edition number and a fair play statement.

All visual details are treated through techniques connected to historical football production. The heritage crest is embroidered using glossy polyester yarn, while sponsors and graphics are reproduced through embroidery or heat-transfer applications. No detail is digitally printed.

The Process

Built Like
The Original.

01

Embroidery

The heritage crest is embroidered using glossy polyester yarn — the same technique used in original production. No shortcuts, no digital printing.

02

Enzyme Wash

Controlled enzyme washing and sun-bleaching create a slightly pre-worn effect — giving the garment the feel of a piece that has lived through the game.

03

Short-Chain

Italian short-chain production connects matter, memory and contemporary responsibility. Recycled fibres and low-impact treatments throughout.

1,897 pieces.
One per year.
